<!-- This file is generated by scripts/generate-agent-skill.mjs. Do not edit manually. -->
## Canonical Vocabulary

- `tone` is semantic color intent: `neutral`, `primary`, `secondary`,
  `tertiary`, or `danger`.
- `status` or status tone is semantic feedback state: `info`, `success`,
  `warning`, or `error`.
- `variant` on controls is visual treatment: `solid`, `soft`, `outline`,
  `ghost`, or `dashed`.
- `variant` on containers is visual treatment: `plain`, `soft`, `outline`, or
  `solid`.
- `surfaceLevel` is neutral page/content depth: `plain`, `surface`, `section`,
  or `inset-high`.
- `shape` is bounded geometry: `rect`, `pill`, or `circle`.

## Separation Rules

- Do not use `tone` for treatment values such as `outline`, `ghost`, `dashed`,
  `soft`, or `surface`.
- Use `danger` for destructive or risky product intent. Use `error` for a failed
  or invalid status that has already happened.
- Use `soft` when the component should keep its semantic tone but lower visual
  emphasis. Use `surfaceLevel` when the component is only expressing neutral
  page or content depth.
- Surface levels are neutral. When a component supports `tone="primary"` or
  `tone="secondary"`, do not rely on `surfaceLevel` to recolor it; use
  container/control `variant` for treatment and `tone` for semantic emphasis.
- `shape="pill"` is a capsule-like radius that preserves natural component
  width. `shape="circle"` is for square icon/avatar surfaces that intentionally
  become round.

## Modifier Classes

Prefer modifiers that preserve the same vocabulary split:

```scss
.pu-component--tone-primary
.pu-component--variant-outline
.pu-component--surface-level-section
.pu-component--shape-pill
```
